body.title: The named checks a ticket's Design-rules field cites
body.statement: Each check has a name a ticket can cite in its **Design rules:** field ('none apply' only after thinking about it), an applies_when, and a failure shape audit-ticket flags at filing time. audit-audits proposes new entries here (rule_promotion) when a finding recurs across tickets.

checks.name: no-sqlite
checks.check_body: no SQLite/file-store fallback in the proposed shape

checks.name: test-plan-or-why-not
checks.check_body: Test plan names specific tests OR states 'no tests because: <reason>'
checks.failure_message: blank test plan — TBD-with-reason is accepted, silence is not

checks.name: affected-files-or-tbd
checks.check_body: Affected files listed, or 'TBD — discovery step in sprint' if genuinely unknown
checks.failure_message: no files named and no discovery step declared

checks.name: two-sided-build
checks.check_body: handler AND skill-consumer paths both in Affected files (or explicit exemption line)

checks.name: validation-observable
checks.check_body: completion criteria name a runtime-observable signal

checks.name: remediation-plan
checks.check_body: rollback path stated before the edit
checks.failure_message: HIGH-inertia change with no way back

checks.name: logging-requirements
checks.applies_when: tickets adding state changes or interface crossings
checks.check_body: the new crossing/state-change names its log point (INFO crossing, DEBUG high-frequency)

checks.name: proof-on-close
checks.check_body: close points at a proof a hollow build couldn't pass, or declares shipped-unproven naming the missing lever

body.why: Named checks make the Design-rules field a real filter instead of prose: the ticket author thinks per-check at filing (cheapest moment), and audit-audits can measure which checks catch and which are dead — a rubric can only improve if its questions have names.
